article_meta.title: SCOTUS Pro-Life Center Takes Its Fight Against Democrat Lawfare To The Supreme Court By: Shawn Fleetwood November 21, 20

reasoning: The summary avoids most of the article’s loaded framing (e.g., it omits terms like “lawfare,” “harass,” and “overtly hostile”) and attributes contested claims to the subjects. However, it adopts one partisan phrasing (“Democrat Attorney General”) and omits the state’s stated rationale for investigating, creating some slant and omission. Overall tone remains largely neutral.

custom_reasoning.spin: Verbs are neutral and contested statements are attributed; no inflammatory action words.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Key claims (targeting, chilling effect) are presented as First Choice/ADF claims or legal questions.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Subjective views are attributed; the model does not assert them as fact.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Avoids emotive language from the source (e.g., “lawfare,” “persecute”).
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks in the model’s voice.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about intent without attribution.
custom_reasoning.slant: Emphasizes the center’s viewpoint and omits the state’s stated allegations that prompted the investigation.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Leaves out New Jersey’s allegation that First Choice misled clients/donors and omits the client testimonial.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Minimal; uses descriptive terms without value-laden adjectives.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Uses the partisan-leaning phrase “Democrat Attorney General” and “pro-life” label; otherwise neutral.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: No undue emphasis on negative outcomes beyond article content.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: No preferential treatment of elite vs grassroots sources detectable.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: More neutral than the source overall but retains a partisan label and lacks some balancing context.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Lists only the publication as a source; misses distinct perspectives (First Choice/ADF, state/AG, legal reference Oyez).
